content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All three amendments verified against source code. fr-16-d6120209: confirmed unconditional sidecar write in commit_snapshot_and_journal_ops with no short-circuit when applied==0 and snapshot unchanged, concurrent readers do react to sidecar presence via XOR-based recovery logic. fr-16-5bcdbbf5: confirmed the first match arm in unwrap_claude_structured_output_payload unconditionally strips single-key {"data": ...} objects without checking the __rb_wrapped sentinel, breaking legitimate contract payloads. fr-16-26354296: confirmed update_status_locked hard-codes "execution started" for all Running transitions while start_bead and build_transition_journal_events correctly use "execution resumed" for paused->running.

## Votes

- `fr-16-d6120209`: Accept
  Verified: commit_snapshot_and_journal_ops (fs.rs:3236-3246) writes the PendingMilestoneStateCommit sidecar unconditionally with no early return when applied==0 and previous_snapshot==next_snapshot. The sister function commit_journal_ops does short-circuit on applied==0, confirming the pattern is known and intentionally applied elsewhere. Concurrent readers invoke recover_pending_state_commit_for_plain_read which reacts to sidecar presence via XOR visibility checks (lines 2730-2734), so unnecessary sidecars cause real recovery overhead in concurrent read paths. Valid robustness defect.
- `fr-16-5bcdbbf5`: Accept
  Verified: unwrap_claude_structured_output_payload (process_backend.rs:1641-1645) has a first match arm that unwraps any single-key object with a "data" key without requiring the __rb_wrapped sentinel. The sentinel is only checked in the second arm for two-key objects. A test at line 2644 confirms this behavior is intentional but it means legitimate contract payloads with schema {"data": <value>} are indistinguishable from Claude transport wrappers and get incorrectly unwrapped. This is a real structured-output correctness regression.
- `fr-16-26354296`: Accept
  Verified: update_status_locked (service.rs:1530-1536) hard-codes reason="execution started" for all MilestoneStatus::Running transitions regardless of source status. Meanwhile start_bead (line 1843) and build_transition_journal_events (line 844) both correctly check if from_status==Paused and use "execution resumed" instead. Since update_status is the general-purpose transition API and paused->running is an explicitly allowed lifecycle transition, callers using the direct API get semantically wrong audit journal entries. Real audit correctness defect.
